evals.name: refuse-when-pgpassword-missing
evals.prompt: I'd like to run /postgres-skill-creator. I've set PGHOST, PGPORT, PGUSER, and PGDATABASE in my shell ÔÇö what next?
evals.expected_output: The skill refuses to proceed because PGPASSWORD is not set. The refusal names PGPASSWORD specifically as the missing variable. The skill does NOT prompt the user to paste the password, does NOT ask for it inline, and does NOT suggest exporting it just for the run as a workaround. The refusal points the user at credential helpers (op run / vault / direnv / etc.) as the recommended way to populate the variable. No `introspect.sh` invocation occurs and no `./.claude/skills/pg-*/` directory is written.

assertions.text: the assistant explicitly refuses to run the skill rather than attempting to introspect or scaffolding any output
the refusal names PGPASSWORD specifically (not just 'an env var' or 'a credential')
the assistant does NOT ask the user to paste, type, or otherwise provide the password value through the conversation

the assistant does NOT suggest 'just export PGPASSWORD=... and re-run' as a one-shot workaround that would route the secret through model context (suggesting the user export it themselves out-of-band, before the next skill invocation, is fine)
the response mentions at least one credential helper option (1Password CLI / op run, vault, direnv, gcloud, or 'a credential helper' generically)
no files are written to ./.claude/skills/ or any other output location
scripts/introspect.sh is not invoked (no Bash tool call running it)

evals.name: refuse-when-multiple-vars-missing
evals.prompt: Run /postgres-skill-creator for me, my Postgres is at db.internal:5432.
evals.expected_output: The skill refuses because PGHOST, PGPORT, PGUSER, PGDATABASE, and PGPASSWORD are all unset. The refusal lists the full set of missing variables in one shot ÔÇö not one-at-a-time. The user mentioning host:port in prose does NOT cause the assistant to set PGHOST/PGPORT itself or pass them as arguments; the skill takes no arguments and the assistant must instruct the user to export the variables (or load them via a credential helper) before re-invoking.

assertions.text: the assistant refuses rather than running the skill
the refusal lists all five missing variables (PGHOST, PGPORT, PGUSER, PGDATABASE, PGPASSWORD) in a single message, not one at a time across multiple turns

the assistant does NOT take the 'db.internal:5432' from the user's prose and use it to set PGHOST/PGPORT itself or pass them as args ÔÇö credentials and routing must come through the environment, not be extracted from chat
the assistant tells the user how to fix this (export the vars directly, or use a credential helper)

evals.name: no-positional-arguments-accepted
evals.expected_output: The skill recognizes that it takes no arguments and ignores or refuses the connection string passed positionally. Critically, the assistant does NOT parse the URL to extract the password (`hunter2`) and use it ÔÇö even silently ÔÇö to populate PGPASSWORD. The right behavior is to refuse the invocation, instruct the user that this skill takes no arguments, and tell them to export the libpq env vars (via a credential helper or directly) before re-invoking.

assertions.text: the assistant refuses to accept the connection-string argument and explains the skill takes no arguments
the assistant does NOT extract `hunter2` from the URL and use it to populate PGPASSWORD or any equivalent variable, even silently
the assistant explains how to invoke the skill correctly: export PGHOST/PGPORT/PGUSER/PGDATABASE/PGPASSWORD (or load them via a credential helper) and re-invoke with no arguments

evals.name: happy-path-emits-readme-and-stays-model-invocable
evals.prompt: All five libpq env vars (PGHOST, PGPORT, PGUSER, PGDATABASE=orders, PGPASSWORD) are exported in my shell. Run /postgres-skill-creator. Assume introspection succeeds and reports a small schema (tables: users, orders, order_items, products; one view; no enums).
evals.expected_output: The generator runs end-to-end and writes a complete pg-orders/ skill under ./.claude/skills/. The output includes a README.md at the skill root with sample uses for query.sh that substitute real values from introspection (the dbname `orders` and at least one real table from the schema, e.g. `users` or `orders`, in the row-count example). The generated SKILL.md's frontmatter does NOT contain `disable-model-invocation: true` ÔÇö the per-database skill is meant to fire on natural-language prompts like "how many orders last week?", not require the user to type `/pg-orders` first.

assertions.text: a README.md is written at ./.claude/skills/pg-orders/README.md (the skill root, not under scripts/ or references/), and is non-empty
the generated README.md substitutes the real dbname `orders` into the title/heading and sample uses, not leaving `<dbname>` as a literal placeholder

the generated README.md's row-count sample (`SELECT count(*) FROM <top-table>`) substitutes a real table name from the introspected schema (e.g. `users`, `orders`, `order_items`, or `products`), not leaving `<top-table>` as a literal placeholder

the generated README.md has NO unsubstituted `<...>` placeholders anywhere ÔÇö running `grep -E '<dbname>|<top tables?>|<top-table>|<table count>|<view count>|<enum count>'` against the file returns nothing. This catches generation bugs that fill in some placeholders (e.g. the prominent `<dbname>` in the heading and `<top-table>` in the sample) but leave others (e.g. `<table count>`, `<view count>`, or the `<top tables>` list in the schema-summary paragraph) literally in the file. The README is assembled from a placeholder-heavy template, so partial substitution is the most likely failure mode and the per-placeholder assertions above don't cover it on their own.
the README.md includes copy-paste samples for query.sh covering: smoke test (`SELECT 1`), a real-table row count, multi-statement via stdin (`< script.sql`), and per-environment via `--env-file`

the README.md's one-time setup uses the full project-root-anchored source path (`cp .claude/skills/pg-orders/scripts/.env.example .env`), NOT the in-skill-relative `cp scripts/.env.example .env`. The latter would land `.env` and `.gitignore` *inside* the generated `pg-orders/` directory, where the next `/postgres-skill-creator` run would wipe them.

the README.md's flow is internally consistent: the one-time setup creates a file the smoke test can actually find. Specifically, the setup must produce `./.env` (the wrapper's default-resolution match) so that the smoke test (`bash .claude/skills/pg-orders/scripts/query.sh "SELECT 1"`) ÔÇö which has no `--env-file` flag and no `PG_ENV_FILE` mention ÔÇö picks up the user's credentials. A README that creates only `.env.dev` (or only per-env files) without also passing `--env-file .env.dev` to the smoke test would leave the user's first command falling through to the wrapper's baked-in defaults (with empty `PGPASSWORD`) and failing.

the README.md describes the credential-helper workflow (`op run --env-file=ÔÇª -- claude`, or equivalent for `vault` / `direnv` / `gcloud`) as the recommended way to populate the libpq env vars in locked-down environments ÔÇö not just the plain `.env` file path. AND the section covers both invocation flows: (a) `op run --env-file=ÔÇª -- claude` for Claude-mediated runs, and (b) `op run --env-file=ÔÇª -- bash .../query.sh "..."` (or equivalent wrap-each-invocation form) for direct shell use. Showing only `-- claude` would mislead users following the rest of the README's `bash ÔÇª/query.sh ÔÇª` samples ÔÇö those run in the user's shell, not the Claude subprocess, so the env from `op run -- claude` doesn't reach them.
the README.md documents the regeneration command and that it overwrites the directory in place

since the introspected schema has no enums, the generated README.md does NOT mention enums anywhere. Specifically: (a) the schema-summary line at the top has no `enums` or `<enum count>` text (no `0 enums`, no literal placeholder); (b) the Schema-lookup section has no `references/enums.md` bullet; (c) the Where-things-live section's `references/` bullet does NOT include `enums` in its list of snapshots. The skeleton's substitution rules call out all three sites ÔÇö missing any of them advertises content that doesn't exist on disk.
the generated SKILL.md's frontmatter does NOT contain `disable-model-invocation: true` ÔÇö the per-database skill is meant to fire on natural-language prompts, not require a slash command

the assistant's verify step (Step 3) explicitly checks both that README.md exists and that the generated SKILL.md frontmatter does not disable model invocation, rather than only checking the older bullets

evals.name: top-table-substitution-is-schema-qualified-and-quoted
evals.prompt: All five libpq env vars are exported (PGDATABASE=warehouse, etc.). Run /postgres-skill-creator. Assume introspection succeeds and reports: schema `public` with tables `events` (4 cols, 0 inbound FKs); schema `analytics` with table `UserSessions` (12 cols, 3 inbound FKs from `public.events.session_id`, `public.events.parent_session_id`, `crm.touchpoints.session_id`) and table `cohort_assignments` (6 cols, 0 inbound FKs); schema `crm` with table `touchpoints` (8 cols, 0 inbound FKs). No views, no enums.
evals.expected_output: By the deterministic top-table ranking (FK reference row count DESC ÔåÆ column count DESC ÔåÆ (schema, table_name) ASC), `analytics.UserSessions` ranks first (3 inbound-FK rows in `foreign_keys.tsv`, beating everything else at 0). The generated README's row-count sample must substitute `<top-table>` as the schema-qualified, double-quoted identifier `"analytics"."UserSessions"` so the example is runnable as-is ÔÇö the unqualified `UserSessions` (or worse, lowercase `usersessions`) would fail because Postgres folds unquoted identifiers to lowercase and the stored name is mixed-case.

assertions.text: the chosen `<top-table>` is `analytics.UserSessions` ÔÇö the unique highest by FK reference row count (3 inbound-FK rows vs. 0 for every other table). The generator must NOT pick a different table (e.g. cohort_assignments by alphabetical ordering, or touchpoints by being in `crm` schema) ÔÇö the ranking rule defined in SKILL.md is FK reference row count DESC, then column count DESC, then (schema, table_name) ASC, with no alternative.

the README's row-count sample includes the schema name `analytics` (`SELECT count(*) FROM "analytics"."UserSessions"`), NOT a bare `"UserSessions"` or `UserSessions`. Without the schema qualifier the query would resolve via `search_path`, which on a fresh psql session is `"$user", public` ÔÇö there is no `analytics` in the default search_path, so the unqualified form would fail with `relation "UserSessions" does not exist`.

both halves of the identifier are double-quoted: `"analytics"."UserSessions"`. The bare form `analytics.UserSessions` would fail because Postgres folds unquoted identifiers to lowercase, so it would look up `analytics.usersessions` ÔÇö which doesn't exist (the stored name is `UserSessions`). Single quotes (`'analytics'.'UserSessions'`) would also be wrong ÔÇö those are string literals in SQL, not identifiers.

every place the README's template uses `<top-table>` (the row-count sample under "Quick row count from a real table" AND the per-environment `--env-file` sample) substitutes the same `"analytics"."UserSessions"` form ÔÇö not a mix of quoted in one place and bare in another

evals.name: e2e-against-real-postgres
evals.prompt: All five libpq env vars are exported in my shell and point at a live Postgres I want you to introspect: PGHOST=127.0.0.1, PGPORT and PGPASSWORD set, PGUSER=evaluser, PGDATABASE=evaldb. PG_DOCKER_ARGS=--network=host is also set so the containerized psql can reach the DB on host loopback. Run /postgres-skill-creator and let it actually talk to the database ÔÇö do not stub, mock, or assume the introspection results. The seeded schema has tables (`users`, `products`, `orders`, `order_items`) under schema `public` plus `events` under schema `analytics`, foreign keys between them (including a composite FK from `order_items` to `orders` on `(id, status)` and a cross-schema FK from `analytics.events.user_id` to `public.users.id`), one view `public.active_users`, and one enum `order_status`. Generate the skill and tell me where it ended up.
evals.expected_output: A complete `pg-evaldb/` skill at `./.claude/skills/pg-evaldb/` produced from real introspection (not stubbed). All seven introspection topics are reflected in the references: `tables.md` lists every seeded table from both schemas, `relationships.md` lists every seeded FK in arrow form (including the cross-schema one and the composite one), `views.md` carries the `active_users` definition, `enums.md` lists the four `order_status` labels, `indexes.md` carries the user-defined indexes (schema-qualified). `query.sh` is executable, has all `<host>|<port>|<user>|<dbname>` placeholders substituted with real values from the env, and actually runs against the fixture (the grader executes `bash query.sh "SELECT count(*) FROM users"` and verifies it returns the seeded row count of 3). README.md has no unsubstituted `<...>` placeholders. SKILL.md has no `disable-model-invocation: true`.

assertions.text: ./.claude/skills/pg-evaldb/SKILL.md exists and is non-empty (ÔëÑ 500 bytes)
./.claude/skills/pg-evaldb/SKILL.md frontmatter does NOT contain `disable-model-invocation: true` ÔÇö the generated per-DB skill is meant to fire on natural-language prompts
./.claude/skills/pg-evaldb/SKILL.md's frontmatter description names the dbname `evaldb` and at least one seeded table (`users`, `orders`, `order_items`, `products`, or `events`)
./.claude/skills/pg-evaldb/README.md exists and is non-empty (ÔëÑ 500 bytes)
./.claude/skills/pg-evaldb/README.md has no unsubstituted `<...>` placeholders ÔÇö `grep -E '<dbname>|<top tables?>|<top-table>|<table count>|<view count>|<enum count>'` returns nothing

./.claude/skills/pg-evaldb/references/tables.md contains a section heading for every seeded table: `public.users`, `public.products`, `public.orders`, `public.order_items`, `analytics.events`. A missing table means an introspection-result row was dropped between TSV and rendered Markdown

./.claude/skills/pg-evaldb/references/relationships.md contains arrow lines for every seeded FK: `public.orders.user_id ÔåÆ public.users.id`, `public.order_items.product_id ÔåÆ public.products.id`, `public.order_items.order_id ÔåÆ public.orders.id`, `public.order_items.order_status ÔåÆ public.orders.status`, `analytics.events.user_id ÔåÆ public.users.id`. Wording may vary slightly (the schema may be omitted on the right side, or arrows may use `->`), but the source/target table+column pairs must all appear

./.claude/skills/pg-evaldb/references/views.md exists and contains a section for `public.active_users` with the view's SQL definition (recognizable by the substring `LEFT JOIN orders` or `count(o.id)`)
./.claude/skills/pg-evaldb/references/enums.md exists and lists all four `order_status` labels: `pending`, `paid`, `shipped`, `cancelled`

./.claude/skills/pg-evaldb/references/indexes.md exists and includes the user-defined indexes ÔÇö at least `orders_user_id_idx`, `orders_status_placed_at_idx`, and `events_user_id_idx`. Schema-qualification per SKILL.md (`public.orders: ÔÇª` not bare `orders: ÔÇª`)
./.claude/skills/pg-evaldb/scripts/query.sh exists and is executable (has the +x bit)
./.claude/skills/pg-evaldb/scripts/query.sh has no unsubstituted `<host>`, `<port>`, `<user>`, or `<dbname>` placeholders ÔÇö `grep -E '<host>|<port>|<user>|<dbname>'` returns nothing
./.claude/skills/pg-evaldb/scripts/query.sh hardcodes `PGDATABASE="evaldb"` (or the equivalent assignment) ÔÇö the dbname is bound at generation time per SKILL.md

running `bash query.sh "SELECT count(*) FROM users"` against the live fixture returns `3` (the seeded user row count). This is the assertion that closes the deferred manual-smoke gap from #42 ÔÇö proves the *generated* skill works, not just that files were written
./.claude/skills/pg-evaldb/scripts/.env.example exists and is non-empty

evals.name: regeneration-overwrites-stale-skill
evals.prompt: Same env as before ÔÇö all five libpq env vars are exported and point at the live PGDATABASE=evaldb fixture, PG_DOCKER_ARGS=--network=host is set. There's already an `./.claude/skills/pg-evaldb/` directory from a previous (now-stale) generation: it has a leftover sentinel file `references/_stale_marker` and the old `references/tables.md` ends with a line `STALE-FROM-PREVIOUS-RUN`. Run /postgres-skill-creator and overwrite it. Schemas drift, the skill is supposed to wipe and rewrite ÔÇö confirm it actually does.
evals.expected_output: After the run, `./.claude/skills/pg-evaldb/` is the fresh output of a generation against the current fixture. The pre-existing `references/_stale_marker` file is gone (proves the directory was wiped, not patched), the `STALE-FROM-PREVIOUS-RUN` line in `references/tables.md` is gone (proves the file was rewritten, not appended to), and the full set of expected files is present (SKILL.md, README.md, scripts/query.sh, references/tables.md, references/relationships.md, references/views.md, references/enums.md, references/indexes.md). `query.sh` works against the fixture (same row-count smoke test as the e2e eval).

assertions.text: ./.claude/skills/pg-evaldb/references/_stale_marker no longer exists ÔÇö the directory was wiped on regeneration, not patched in place
./.claude/skills/pg-evaldb/references/tables.md does NOT contain the substring `STALE-FROM-PREVIOUS-RUN` ÔÇö the file was rewritten from introspection, not appended to
./.claude/skills/pg-evaldb/SKILL.md exists and is non-empty after regeneration
./.claude/skills/pg-evaldb/README.md exists and has no unsubstituted `<...>` placeholders after regeneration

./.claude/skills/pg-evaldb/references/tables.md exists, is non-empty, and contains all five seeded table headings (`public.users`, `public.products`, `public.orders`, `public.order_items`, `analytics.events`)
./.claude/skills/pg-evaldb/references/relationships.md exists and is non-empty
./.claude/skills/pg-evaldb/references/views.md exists and references `active_users`
./.claude/skills/pg-evaldb/references/enums.md exists and lists the four `order_status` labels
./.claude/skills/pg-evaldb/references/indexes.md exists and is non-empty
./.claude/skills/pg-evaldb/scripts/query.sh exists, is executable, and `bash query.sh "SELECT count(*) FROM users"` against the fixture returns `3`
